apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: canary-stable
  namespace: demo
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - host: canary.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /health
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: stable-echo
            port:
              number: 80
      - path: /
        pathType: Exact
        backend:
          service:
            name: stable-echo
            port:
              number: 80
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: canary-canary
  namespace: demo
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header: "X-Canary"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header-value: "always"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - host: canary.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Exact
        backend:
          service:
            name: canary-echo
            port:
              number: 80
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: canary-echo
  namespace: demo
  labels:
    app: canary-echo
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: canary-echo
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: canary-echo
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: http-echo
        image: hashicorp/http-echo:0.2.3
        args:
          - "-text=canary"
          - "-listen=:5678"
        ports:
          - containerPort: 5678
            name: http
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: canary-echo
  namespace: demo
  labels:
    app: canary-echo
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: canary-echo
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 80
    targetPort: 5678
    protocol: TCP

# Two backends (stable and canary) exist in the demo namespace. A canary

# backend and all other requests go to stable.

params:
  host:
    default: canary.example.com
    description: Hostname both the stable and canary Ingress serve.
  header_name:
    default: X-Canary
    description: Request header that selects the canary backend.
  header_value:
    default: always
    description: Header value that routes traffic to the canary backend.
  stable_body:
    default: stable
    description: Response body returned by the stable backend.
  canary_body:
    default: canary
    description: Response body returned by the canary backend.
  curl_pod_name:
    default: curl-test
    description: Name of the in-cluster pod used to issue test requests.

  - stable: returns "{{params.stable_body}}"
  - canary: returns "{{params.canary_body}}"

  A canary Ingress exists, but canary traffic is not reaching the canary
  backend.

  Your task:
  1) Requests with header "{{params.header_name}}: {{params.header_value}}" must route to the canary backend.
  2) Requests without that header must route to the stable backend.
